title: Game Objects — Classes
description: Convert your game data into proper classes. Create a Room class with name, description, and items attributes plus an __init__ and __str__ method. Create an Item class with name, item_type, and value attributes. Create a Player class with name, health, max_health, and inventory attributes, plus a __str__ method that shows the player's status.
starter_code:
# Text Adventure — Step 8: Game Objects with Classes
# Convert data structures into proper classes.

class Item:
    """Represents an item in the game world."""
    def __init__(self, name, item_type, value):
        # Store name, item_type, and value as attributes
        pass

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name} ({self.item_type})"


class Room:
    """Represents a room in the game world."""
    def __init__(self, name, description):
        # Store name and description; initialize items as empty list
        pass

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name}: {self.description}"

    def add_item(self, item):
        """Add an item to this room."""
        pass

    def remove_item(self, item_name):
        """Remove and return an item by name, or return None."""
        pass


class Player:
    """Represents the player character."""
    def __init__(self, name, health=100):
        # Store name, health, max_health (same as health), inventory (empty list)
        pass

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name} (HP: {self.health}/{self.max_health})"

    def pick_up(self, item):
        """Add an item to the player's inventory."""
        pass

    def drop_item(self, item_name):
        """Remove and return an item by name, or return None."""
        pass


# Build the game world using classes
sword = Item('iron_sword', 'weapon', 10)
potion = Item('health_potion', 'potion', 25)
key = Item('rusty_key', 'key', 'treasure_room')

entrance = Room('entrance', 'You stand at the entrance of a dark cave.')
hallway = Room('hallway', 'A long hallway with torches on the walls.')
treasure = Room('treasure_room', 'A glittering room full of gold and jewels.')

entrance.add_item(sword)
hallway.add_item(potion)
treasure.add_item(key)

hero = Player('Hero', 100)

# Test
print(entrance)
print(f"Items in entrance: {[str(i) for i in entrance.items]}")
hero.pick_up(entrance.remove_item('iron_sword'))
print(hero)
print(f"Inventory: {[str(i) for i in hero.inventory]}")
test_code:
assert isinstance(Room, type), 'Room should be a class'
assert isinstance(Item, type), 'Item should be a class'
assert isinstance(Player, type), 'Player should be a class'
test_item = Item('test_gem', 'key', 'door')
assert test_item.name == 'test_gem', f"Item.name should be 'test_gem', got {test_item.name}"
assert test_item.item_type == 'key', f"Item.item_type should be 'key', got {test_item.item_type}"
assert test_item.value == 'door', f"Item.value should be 'door', got {test_item.value}"
assert 'test_gem' in str(test_item), f"str(Item) should contain name, got: {str(test_item)}"
test_room = Room('lobby', 'A grand lobby.')
assert test_room.name == 'lobby', f"Room.name should be 'lobby', got {test_room.name}"
assert test_room.description == 'A grand lobby.', f"Room.description wrong"
assert isinstance(test_room.items, list), 'Room.items should be a list'
assert len(test_room.items) == 0, 'Room.items should start empty'
test_room.add_item(test_item)
assert len(test_room.items) == 1, 'Room should have 1 item after add'
removed = test_room.remove_item('test_gem')
assert removed is not None, 'remove_item should return the item'
assert removed.name == 'test_gem', 'Removed item should match'
assert len(test_room.items) == 0, 'Room should be empty after remove'
assert test_room.remove_item('nothing') is None, 'Removing nonexistent should return None'
test_player = Player('Tester', 80)
assert test_player.name == 'Tester', f"Player.name should be 'Tester', got {test_player.name}"
assert test_player.health == 80, f"Player.health should be 80, got {test_player.health}"
assert test_player.max_health == 80, f"Player.max_health should be 80, got {test_player.max_health}"
assert isinstance(test_player.inventory, list), 'Player.inventory should be a list'
test_player.pick_up(test_item)
assert len(test_player.inventory) == 1, 'Inventory should have 1 item'
dropped = test_player.drop_item('test_gem')
assert dropped is not None, 'drop_item should return the item'
assert len(test_player.inventory) == 0, 'Inventory should be empty after drop'
assert 'Tester' in str(test_player), 'str(Player) should contain name'
acceptance_criteria: Room class has name, description, and items attributes
Item class has name, item_type, and value attributes
Player class has name, health, max_health, and inventory attributes
All classes have working __init__ and __str__ methods
hints: In __init__, use self.name = name to store attributes
Initialize lists as self.items = [] (not a mutable default argument!)
For remove_item/drop_item: loop with enumerate, pop by index, return the item
Player.__init__ can set self.max_health = health so both start equal
